_note: Derived (not verbatim) acceptance items tagged Decision basis (read by the regulator to decide; stated in the ER) vs. Backup (owner-controlled records cited from the ER but not reproduced in it).7) for the ESP ER, built from NUREG-1555 (2000) 'II. Acceptance Criteria' governing-requirement lists, read with RG 4.2 and the NR GEIS tiering. Each test is answerable yes/no by pointing at a stated value, a cited document/permit/consultation, or a value-vs-limit comparison. The authorities list restates the source's cited requirements. Confirm against the source before filing. Numeric thresholds that could not be verified against a readable source this session were removed and replaced with references to the governing regulation (10 CFR 100.3/100.21/100.23, RG 1.208, CEQ EJ guidance); the applicant states the actual value from the current rule.

tests: Does the ER state the applicant, reactor type, and site location (State, county, and site-center coordinates)? (Y/N)
Does the ER cite where the environmental documents are publicly available? (Y/N)
authorities: NR GEIS 1.4 (Implementation of the Rule; the GEIS is the ER-streamlining basis, not an issue conclusion)
10 CFR 51, Subpart A (procedural matters; availability of environmental documents)

tests: Does the ER contain a table listing every Federal permit, license, approval, and entitlement required? (Y/N)
Does each row state the compliance/consultation status (not started / initiated on [date] / in progress / complete)? (Y/N)

10 CFR 51.45(d) (list all Federal authorizations; state compliance status)
10 CFR 51.70 (staff independent evaluation of information)
10 CFR 51.71 (EIS lists all Federal authorizations)
